title: willughbeia ovatifolia
intro: Willughbeia ovatifolia, also known as the 'Papuan willughbeia', is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, native to New Guinea. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Willughbeia ovatifolia include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to act as an antimicrobial agent. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tincture or an infusion by steeping the leaves in hot water, and is used in herbal medicine to alleviate various health conditions.
